hero.headline: Amrita Online MBA in Marketing: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: NAAC A++ | NIRF #8 University 2025 | UGC-DEB approved | WES recognised

sections.tldr: Amrita Online MBA in Marketing covers digital marketing, retail marketing, data-driven marketing, marketing research, and CRM. Total tuition Rs 2,20,000 (semester fee Rs 55,000). Plus Rs 700 registration and Rs 11,000 exam fees. Total cost of programme Rs 2,31,700. Zero-cost EMI available directly through the university. NAAC A++. NIRF #8 University 2025. WES recognised for USA and Canada.

about.body:
Marketing is among the most widely pursued MBA specialisations in India, and Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Online's curriculum stands out for its combination of digital marketing, retail, data-driven marketing, and CRM - covering both digital-first brand roles and traditional channel management in one specialisation.

The Sem 3-4 curriculum moves from channel and digital strategy foundations into data-driven decision-making and customer relationship management. This combination prepares graduates for brand management, digital marketing, and marketing analytics roles at consumer goods companies, e-commerce platforms, and agencies.

Amrita holds NIRF #8 University 2025 and NAAC A++ - India's highest accreditation grade. WES recognition gives graduates formal degree equivalence in the USA and Canada, relevant for candidates targeting international brand management or marketing consulting roles. The Study Abroad option includes exchanges at Oakland University (USA), University of New Mexico (USA), Institut Mines Telecom (France), and University of Barcelona (Spain).

EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 subjects are shared across all Amrita MBA specialisations. Sem 3-4 below are specific to the Marketing specialisation.

subjects.name: International Business
subjects.desc: Global trade frameworks, cross-border market entry strategies, FDI analysis, and managing international operations.

subjects.name: Business Analytics and Business Modelling
subjects.desc: Quantitative business modelling, regression analysis, forecasting techniques, and data-driven decision-making frameworks.

subjects.name: Digital Marketing
subjects.desc: Search engine marketing, social media advertising, content strategy, SEO and SEM fundamentals, and performance marketing metrics.

subjects.name: Retail Marketing
subjects.desc: Retail store strategy, shopper behaviour, category management, omni-channel retail execution, and visual merchandising frameworks.

subjects.name: Education for Life
subjects.desc: Soft skills development, mindfulness, health and wellbeing, and holistic personal effectiveness for professionals.

subjects.name: Environment Sustainability and Governance
subjects.desc: ESG reporting frameworks, corporate sustainability strategy, carbon accounting basics, and governance obligations.

subjects.name: Project
subjects.desc: Applied industry marketing project integrating specialisation knowledge with real-world brand or campaign problem-solving.

subjects.name: Data-Driven Marketing
subjects.desc: Customer analytics, attribution modelling, A/B testing, marketing mix modelling, and using analytics platforms for campaign optimisation.

subjects.name: Marketing Research
subjects.desc: Quantitative and qualitative research design, survey methodology, conjoint analysis, focus group facilitation, and market sizing approaches.

subjects.name: CRM
subjects.desc: Customer lifecycle management, CRM platform configuration, loyalty programme design, customer segmentation, and churn prediction basics.

whoHires.body:
Marketing MBA graduates from Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Online are hired across India's consumer and digital marketing sector:

**Industries:** FMCG brand management, e-commerce marketing, digital agencies, retail chains, BFSI marketing, edtech growth, healthcare marketing, and B2B marketing

**Entry-level roles:** Brand Executive, Digital Marketing Executive, Marketing Analyst, CRM Associate, Retail Marketing Executive, Social Media Analyst

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Brand Manager, Digital Marketing Manager, Category Manager, Marketing Analytics Manager, CRM Manager, Trade Marketing Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** VP Marketing, Chief Marketing Officer, Head of Digital, Brand Director, Marketing Director (FMCG), VP Growth

**Top hiring organisations:** HUL, P&G India, Coca-Cola India, Marico, Godrej Consumer, Amazon India marketing, Flipkart growth, Nykaa, Meesho, Swiggy, Zomato marketing, Myntra brand, JWT, Ogilvy India, and BFSI marketing teams across HDFC Life, SBI Life, and Bajaj Finserv

skills.body:
The Marketing specialisation builds skills that brand management, digital marketing, and marketing analytics employers test at executive and manager levels:

**Technical skills:**
- Digital Marketing: Google Ads campaign setup, Meta advertising basics, SEO audit methodology, content calendar management, and performance marketing KPI tracking
- Data-Driven Marketing: Google Analytics 4 interpretation, attribution model comparison, A/B test design and significance testing, and marketing mix model reading
- CRM: Salesforce and Zoho CRM basics, customer segmentation logic, RFM analysis, loyalty tier design, and email automation workflow mapping
- Retail Marketing: planogram design basics, category management P&L, shopper journey mapping, and retail promotional ROI analysis

**Applied skills:**
- Marketing Research: primary research design, brand tracking study methodology, conjoint analysis interpretation, and NPS programme management
- Brand Management: brand architecture frameworks, positioning statement writing, brand audit methodology, and campaign briefing for creative agencies

**Soft skills:** Presenting brand strategy to senior leadership using data, managing agency relationships and campaign quality, communicating market research findings to non-marketing stakeholders, and building marketing literacy in cross-functional product and sales teams.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Amrita Online MBA Marketing against peer programmes:

**vs Amity Online MBA Marketing and Sales Management:** Amity at NIRF #22 has a marketing-sales-management spec with a broad curriculum covering sales alongside marketing. Amity is a strong option with established brand recognition. Amrita at NAAC A++ and NIRF #8 carries higher institutional weight, and WES recognition gives Amrita graduates a formal credential advantage for international brand or digital marketing roles.

**vs NMIMS Online MBA Marketing Management:** NMIMS is a premium brand in Indian management education. NMIMS Online's marketing-management curriculum is well-regarded for the NMIMS name. Amrita at NIRF #8 (university rank) competes strongly; NAAC A++ and WES recognition differentiate Amrita for candidates targeting North American careers or institutional credential filters.

**vs Chandigarh University Online MBA Marketing:** CU at NIRF #19 with PwC co-teaching and Harvard certificate offers strong value at Rs 1,65,000 (Tier 1). For candidates wanting PwC credentials at a lower fee, CU is a strong alternative. Amrita at Rs 2,20,000 adds NAAC A++ (vs CU's A+), NIRF #8 (vs #19), and WES recognition - justified for candidates where these filters apply.

**vs Shoolini University Online MBA Marketing Management:** Shoolini at NAAC A and NIRF #92 has a marketing-management spec. For candidates who require a higher NIRF rank or NAAC A++ for employer credential filters, Amrita is the clear upgrade.

fees.body:
The Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Online MBA in Marketing carries a tuition fee of Rs 2,20,000 total across 4 semesters (2-year programme).

**Mandatory fees (all students, disclose before enrolment):**
- Registration fee: Rs 700 (one-time)
- Examination fee: Rs 2,750 per semester x 4 = Rs 11,000 total
- **Total cost of programme: Rs 2,20,000 + Rs 700 + Rs 11,000 = Rs 2,31,700**

**Payment options for Indian students:**
- Semester fee: Rs 55,000 per semester
- Annual fee with 5% discount: Rs 1,04,500 per year
- Zero-cost EMI: available directly through Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham (contact counsellor for current monthly instalment and tenure options)

**Foreign national students:**
Contact our counsellor for foreign national tuition track applicable to the Marketing specialisation.

Fees listed are indicative. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Amrita each quarter. Please reconfirm with our counsellor before any payment.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive an Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham MBA degree certificate with Marketing noted on the marksheet, issued under UGC-DEB 2020 online mode standards.

NAAC A++ and NIRF #8 University 2025 are the strongest institutional credentials in India's online MBA space. WES recognition gives formal degree equivalence in the USA and Canada - useful for candidates targeting international marketing, brand management, or digital agency roles in North America. DigiLocker verification is available.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Online MBA Marketing alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Pooja S.
items.liked: Data-Driven Marketing subject gave me the attribution and A/B testing frameworks my agency team needed to improve client reporting
items.disliked: Digital Marketing content covered Google Ads and Meta well but LinkedIn B2B advertising and programmatic basics were light
items.body: I work at a digital marketing agency managing FMCG clients. The data-driven marketing and CRM subjects were directly applicable to the client retention and campaign analytics work I do daily. NAAC A++ from NIRF #8 is the strongest credential available in online MBA space - it cleared every employer filter when I moved to a larger agency. WES recognition is a bonus for the international client work I do. Zero-cost EMI from the university was easy to set up.

items.name: Ravi M.
items.liked: CRM subject content was thorough - covered the full lifecycle from segmentation through churn prediction in a business-applicable way
items.disliked: Retail Marketing content felt South India oriented - more national chain and e-commerce retail case studies would improve relevance for non-metro markets
items.body: I manage a brand team at an FMCG company. The marketing research and CRM subjects gave me frameworks I now apply in our quarterly brand health tracking work. NAAC A++ is a credential that carries weight in FMCG interviews - several hiring managers mentioned it directly. Study Abroad option was available but I did not need it for my career path.

items.name: Sneha K.
items.liked: Marketing Research subject directly improved my ability to design and present consumer insights studies
items.disliked: Counsellor follow-up for Delhi NCR admission support was inconsistent compared to what Bengaluru-based alumni reported
items.body: I transitioned from a sales role to a brand management track using this MBA. The digital marketing, data-driven marketing, and marketing research subjects were exactly the combination I needed. NAAC A++ from NIRF #8 cleared the institutional filter at every FMCG firm I interviewed with. Total fee of Rs 2,31,700 with zero-cost EMI was manageable on my existing salary.

faqs.question: What makes Amrita Marketing MBA different from standard online marketing programmes?
faqs.answer: The combination of NAAC A++ (India's highest accreditation grade), NIRF #8 University 2025, and WES recognition differentiates Amrita from all other online marketing MBA options. The curriculum covers digital marketing, retail, data-driven analytics, marketing research, and CRM in one specialisation - a practical mix for brand management, agency, and marketing analytics careers. No other online MBA university in India holds NAAC A++.

faqs.question: Does the Marketing MBA cover digital marketing tools and platforms?
faqs.answer: Yes. The Digital Marketing subject covers Google Ads and Meta advertising, SEO/SEM frameworks, content strategy, and performance marketing KPI tracking. The Data-Driven Marketing subject covers Google Analytics 4, A/B testing, attribution modelling, and marketing mix modelling. The CRM subject covers Salesforce and Zoho CRM basics, email automation, and customer segmentation - giving graduates practical fluency with the tools most marketing employers use.

faqs.question: What is the total cost of the Amrita Marketing MBA including all fees?
faqs.answer: Total cost of programme is Rs 2,31,700 - comprising Rs 2,20,000 tuition, Rs 700 one-time registration, and Rs 11,000 examination fees (Rs 2,750 per semester across 4 semesters). Zero-cost EMI is available directly through Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham. Annual fee with 5% discount is Rs 1,04,500 per year.

faqs.question: Is this MBA useful for transitioning from sales to brand management?
faqs.answer: Yes. The Retail Marketing and CRM subjects are directly applicable for sales-to-brand transitions, covering channel management, customer lifecycle strategy, and category management P&L. The Marketing Research and Data-Driven Marketing subjects add the analytical skills that brand management roles require beyond sales. NAAC A++ and NIRF #8 give the credential credibility at FMCG and consumer brand employers.

faqs.question: Does WES recognition help for marketing careers outside India?
faqs.answer: WES formally assesses the Amrita MBA as equivalent to a Canadian and American master's degree. For marketing professionals targeting North American brand management, digital marketing agency, or marketing consulting roles, this formal equivalence removes the credential evaluation barrier. For purely India-based marketing careers, WES recognition is an additional credential that adds value without requiring any additional action.

chooseIf: You want a Marketing MBA from India's only NAAC A++ online university with NIRF #8 ranking.
WES recognition for USA and Canada career applications or Canadian PR is a priority.
Your target employers in FMCG, e-commerce, or digital agencies filter by NAAC grade or NIRF rank.
You want digital marketing, retail, data-driven analytics, and CRM in one marketing specialisation.
consider: You want PwC co-teaching and Harvard certificate at a lower price point (consider Chandigarh University Marketing).
You want a marketing-plus-sales curriculum (consider Amity Online Marketing and Sales Management).
Your budget is under Rs 2,00,000 and NAAC A++ is not a filter for your target employers.
